title: Persian Gulf Fertilizer Crisis — Global Food Supply Implications
author: IOWN Research
summary: The closure of the Strait of Hormuz has taken 30-35% of global ammonia and 11% of urea production offline — the most significant fertilizer disruption since WWII. Analysis of food supply implications, US insulation, and investment opportunities across fertilizers, ag equipment, and energy.
